title: Capsella macroclada

taxonomy: Capsella macroclada is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Capsella, which is a group of small, annual plants. The species is also known as shepard's cress, and its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Brassicales. Capsella macroclada is native to Europe and Asia, and its classification is used to identify and distinguish it from other similar species. The plant's classification is an important part of its scientific identity and is used by botanists and researchers to study and understand its characteristics.
